## reference · refactoring ui practical visual design hierarchy depth color spacing typography
* Start with a feature, not a layout, and design the shell and navigation later
* Design in grayscale to use spacing, contrast, and size to do the heavy lifting
* Don't over-invest in details like fonts and colors early on, use low-fidelity sketches and wireframes
* Work in cycles: design a feature simply, build it, fix errors
* Choose a personality for your design: font choice, color, border radius, and language
* Limit your choices: define systems in advance, use a working palette color, and limit yourself to it
* Establish a visual hierarchy: use font weight, color, and size to create hierarchy
* Limit yourself to 2-3 colors and font weights to define hierarchy
* Use white space effectively: start with too much white space, then remove until happy
* Establish a spacing and sizing system: create a constrained spacing/sizing system based on a base unit
* Use a constrained type scale to speed up font size decisions and add consistency
* Pick scale values by hand instead of using a mathematical ratio
* Use high-quality fonts: safe bets are neutral sans-serifs and system fonts
* Keep line length between 45-75 characters for optimal readability
* Align mixed font sizes by their baselines, not centers
* Use HSL (hue, saturation, lightness) instead of HEX and RGB for color
* Aim for 10+ shades of greys, primary colors, and accent colors
* Define color shades upfront instead of generating on the fly
* Choose the base color first, then pick the lightest and darkest shades
* Use perceived brightness to calculate the brightness of a color
* Rotate the hue to make the color pop more
* Don't rely solely on color to convey information, add visual cues
* Mimic how light works in the real world to create depth
* Use shadows to convey elevation, but don't overdo it
* Define a fixed set of shadows as an elevation system for consistency
* Create depth with color: lighter colors feel closer
* Use high-quality photos, and add an overlay to background images to get readable headlines
* Don't scale up icons, use a more detailed icon instead
Sources: https://www.sglavoie.com/posts/2023/09/09/book-summary-refactoring-ui/
